The systems and networking group at UCSD engages in a wide range of experimental and theoretical research. Our current projects span fault-tolerant networks and systems, high-speed router design, storage system design, network measurement and traffic analysis, peer-to-peer system design, network security, mobile code architectures, high-performance cluster computing, and wireless networking. Our group consists of eight core faculty, a range of affiliated faculty (in a broad range of areas including security, machine learning and programming languages) and over 50 graduate students and research staff.

[All Alumni]
Recent News

June 5— We have two winners at the CSE Department Doctoral Awards! Evan Johnson (now an assisstant professor at NYU) received the dissertation award ("Verifiably Secure Software Sandboxing") and Sumanth Rao received the Service and Leadership award (with a RECORD 15 nominations!). Congratgs to both Sumanth and Evan!

June 5— Congratulations to Alisha Ukani who successfully defended her thesis, "Empirically Analyzing Web Privacy Defenses" today! Alisha will be soon head to UCSD North (UW) where she will commence post-doctoral studies. Good luck, Alisha!

June 4— Today, Rukshani Athapathu successfully defended her dissertation "Toward Efficient and Fragmentation-Aware Communication in Reconfigurable Networks". Congrats to Dr. Athapathu and good luck!

May 18— Congratulations to Sumanth Rao, Ye Shu, Stefan Savage, Aaron Schulman, Geoff Voelker and Enze Liu for reciving the IEEE Distuguished Paper award for their paper Lost in Translation: Text Message Spoofing via Email!

May 3— Congratulations to Amy Ousterhout and Daniel Grier as they became parents of newly arrived baby Zachary! (We are all very jealous of his car seat.)

March 13— Congratulations to Eric Mugnier who successfully defended his thesis, "Democratizing Practical Formal Verification for Real Systems Through a User-Centered Approach", on Friday the 13th! Dr. Mugnier will soon be leaving us to join many of our other alumns at Meta. Congrats Eric!

December 2— Good things come in pairs. Congratulations to Alex Yen who successfully defended his thesis today, "Non-Traditional Infrastructure for Wide-Area Wireless Connectivity". Alex wins the award for the most diverse assortment of communication platforms, including planes, trains, and balloons. Congrats Alex!

November 13— Congratulations to George Sullivan who successfully defended his thesis today, "Passive Aggressive: Network Attacks via Passive Analysis". Today therefore also marks George's tearful/joyful goodbye to the hygiene server. Congrats George!

October 13— Congrats to Wenyi "Morty" Zhang, Annie Dai, Keegan Ryan, Dave Levin, Nadia Heninger and Aaron Schulman for their amazing CCS paper featured in today's Wired Story "Satellites are Leaking the World's Secrets: Calls, Texts, Military and Corporate Data"! More information can be found at the project page.

August 19— Congratulations to Ben Du who defended his dissertation today, entitled "Evaluating the Effectiveness of BGP Origin Validation Mechanisms Using Public Data Sources"! After 13 long years at UCSD, Ben will be (ahem) "moving" north to start a PostDoc at UCLA. It is hard to imagine what SysNet and CAIDA will look like without him, but we suspect he will not be entirely absent. Congratulations, Ben!

August 13— CSE alumni Stephen Checkoway, Damon McCoy, Danny Anderson, along with Brian Kantor, Hovav Shacham, and Stefan Savage at UCSD together with Yoshi Kohno, Karl Koscher, Alexei Czeskis and Franziska Roesner at UW received the 2025 USENIX Security Test of Time award today for their 2011 paper Comprehensive Experimental Analyses of Automotive Attack Surfaces. This paper, along with its predecessor (which also received the Test-of-time award at IEEE in 2020), is credited with helping to establish the field of modern automotive security. This body of work also received the AAAS Golden Goose Award in 2021. Congratulations to everyone on such an outstanding accomplishment!

August 11— Congratulations to Enze "Alex" Liu who defended his dissertation today, entitled "Security Vulnerabilities in Service Composition"! Alex has been a cornerstone of the sysnetsec groups, promoting community within the department, supporting and guiding many other students, and blazing trails with his research. Dr. Liu soon transitions to CMU as a postdoc. Congrats, Alex!

August 1— Today Amanda Tomlinson successfully defended her dissertation, "Leveraging the Variability of Computing Resources and Renewable Energy to Lower the Carbon Footprint of Computing", to a very energetic crowd. Dr. Tomlinson will soon be leaving us to join Google where she will continue to innocently invite colleagues to play a quick game of pool. Congratulations Amanda!

July 16— Congrats to Amy Ousterhout for receiving an inaugural "Google ML and Systems Junior Faculty Award" in recognition of the significance and promise of her work in Operating Systems and Networks. More on the new award, given to leading faculty at 27 US universities, in a Google blog post from sysnet alumn Amin Vahdat.
[All News]
Recent Publications

WarrInt: Integrity Validation for Criminal Legal Process, Alisha Ukani, Ross Greer, Katherine Izhikevich, Earlence Fernandes, Alex C. Snoeren, and Stefan Savage, Proceedings of the 35th USENIX Security Symposium, Baltimore, MD, August 2026.

Countering Fabricated Legal Documents: Aligning Verification Attention to Salient Objects through Zero-Shot Detection of Confounding or Obscuring Symbols, Vrishan Inukollu, Porfirio Montoya, Ross Greer, Alisha Ukani, Katherine Izhikevich, Earlence Fernandes, Stefan Savage, and Alex C. Snoeren, Proceedings of the From Perception to Persuasion: Challenges and Advances in Misinformation Detection in Society (PP-MisDet) Workshop at the Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Conference (CVPR), Denver, CO, June 2026.

Understanding Server-side Commercial Fingerprinting, Elisa Luo, Tom Ritter, Stefan Savage, and Geoffrey M. Voelker, Proceedings of the Web Conference (WWW), Dubai, UAE, June 2026.

Lost in Translation: Text Message Spoofing via Email, Sumanth Rao, Ye Shu, Stefan Savage, Aaron Schulman, Geoffrey M. Voelker, and Enze Liu, Proceedings of the IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy, May 2026. (Distinguished Paper Award).

Different Policies for Different NodeBs: Comparing Downlink Schedulers in Cellular Base Stations, Zesen Zhang, Jon Larrea, Jarrett Huddleston, Haoran Wan, Ricky K. P. Mok, Bradley Huffaker, kc Claffy, Kyle Jamieson, Alexander Marder, and Aaron Schulman, Proceedings of the Passive and Active Measurement Conference (PAM), Virtual, March 2026.

CRASHCART: Truckinâ in a Backup System to Revive Hospitals During Computer Outages, Almog Bar-Yossef, Kartikeyan Subramanyam, Jonathon Guthrie, Alex Gao, Christian Dameff, Jeff Tully, and Aaron Schulman, Proceedings of ACSAC Workshop on Cybersecurity in Healthcare (HealthSec), Honolulu, Hawaii, December 2025.

Canvassing the Fingerprinters: Characterizing Canvas Fingerprinting Use Across the Web, Elisa Luo, Tom Ritter, Stefan Savage, and Geoffrey M. Voelker, Proceedings of the ACM Internet Measurement Conference (IMC), Madison, WI, October 2025.

Somesite I Used To Crawl: Awareness, Agency and Efficacy in Protecting Content Creators From AI Crawlers, Enze Liu, Elisa Luo, Shawn Shan, Geoffrey M. Voelker, Ben Y. Zhao, and Stefan Savage, Proceedings of the ACM Internet Measurement Conference (IMC), Madison, WI, October 2025.

Tock: From Research to Securing 10 Million Computers, Leon Schuermann, Bradford Campbell, Branden Ghena, Philip Levis, Amit Levy, and Pat Pannuto, Proceedings of the 31st ACM Symposium on Operating Systems Principles (SOSP), Seoul, Republic of Korea, October 2025.

TickTock: Verified Isolation in a Production Embedded OS, Vivien Rindisbacher, Evan Johnson, Nico Lehmann, Tyler Potyondy, Pat Pannuto, Stefan Savage, Deian Stefan, and Ranjit Jhala, Proceedings of the 31st ACM Symposium on Operating Systems Principles (SOSP), Seoul, Republic of Korea, October 2025.

Pesto: Cooking up High Performance BFT Queries, Florian Suri-Payer, Neil Giridharan, Liam Arzola, Shir Cohen, Lorenzo Alvisi, and Natacha Crooks, Proceedings of the 31st ACM Symposium on Operating Systems Principles (SOSP), Seoul, Republic of Korea, October 2025.

Donât Look Up: There Are Sensitive Internal Links in the Clear on GEO Satellites, Wenyi Zhang, Annie Dai, Keegan Ryan, Dave Levin, Nadia Heninger, and Aaron Schulman, Proceedings of the ACM Conference on Computer and Communications Security (CCS), Taipei, Taiwan, October 2025. (Distinguished Paper Award).

Local Frames: Exploiting Inherited Origins to Bypass Content Blockers, Alisha Ukani, Hamed Haddadi, Alex C. Snoeren, and Peter Snyder, Proceedings of the ACM Conference on Computer and Communications Security (CCS), Taipei, Taiwan, October 2025.

A Developer-Friendly Application-Integrated Far Memory, Anil Yelam, Stewart Grant, Nadav Amit, Radhika Niranjan Mysore, Amy Ousterhout, Marcos K. Aguilera, and Alex C. Snoeren, USENIX ;login:, 2025.
